target.title: fix: reject hooks token reuse with gateway password auth
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: If `hooks.token` is reused as `gateway.auth.password`, an attacker who knows the hook token can escalate from hook-only ingress into full Gateway operator HTTP access. That enables privileged tool execution and other trusted-operator actions outside the intended hook dispatcher boundary.
- Why this matters now: If `hooks.token` is reused as `gateway.auth.password`, an attacker who knows the hook token can escalate from hook-only ingress into full Gateway operator HTTP access. That enables privileged tool execution and other trusted-operator actions outside the intended hook dispatcher boundary.
- Intended outcome: If `hooks.token` is reused as `gateway.auth.password`, an attacker who knows the hook token can escalate from hook-only ingress into full Gateway operator HTTP access. That enables privileged tool execution and other trusted-operator actions outside the intended hook dispatcher boundary.
- Intentionally out of scope: unrelated auth, gateway, hook, or configuration behavior.
- Success looks like: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` reports `passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)` with no new failures.
- Reviewer focus: the startup auth guard and regression coverage for credential reuse.

## Linked context

- Closes #87376
- Related #
- Requested by a maintainer or owner: No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: If `hooks.token` is reused as `gateway.auth.password`, an attacker who knows the hook token can escalate from hook-only ingress into full Gateway operator HTTP access. That enables privileged tool execution and other trusted-operator actions outside the intended hook dispatcher boundary.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout using the managed fix worktree.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` reported `passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)`.
- Observed result after fix: passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)
- What was not tested: interactive UI flows outside the startup-auth regression path.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: full `pnpm test` has pre-existing upstream failures recorded by the submit gate; no new failures were introduced by this branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): see the linked issue reproduction and analysis.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
- Regression coverage added or updated: startup-auth tests now reject `hooks.token` reuse with gateway token, password, or trusted-proxy auth.
- What failed before this fix, if known: configurations could reuse the hooks token as the gateway auth secret in password and trusted-proxy modes.
- If no test was added, why not: N/A; regression coverage was updated.
- Validation result: passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)
- AI-assisted: yes
- Model/provider: github-copilot/gpt-5.3-codex
- CVSS v3.1: 10.0 (Critical)
- CVSS v4.0: 10.0 (Critical)
- Changed files:
- `src/gateway/startup-auth.test.ts` (+22/-23)
- `src/gateway/startup-auth.ts` (+9/-5)

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes - invalid gateway auth configurations that reuse `hooks.token` are now rejected at startup.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes - affected deployments must use a distinct gateway token or password.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes - the gateway auth guard now enforces credential separation.
- Highest-risk area: rejecting an existing but unsafe credential reuse configuration.
- Risk mitigation: `pnpm exec oxlint src/ && pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` reported `passed (with pre-existing baseline failures)` and the changed-file scope is limited to startup auth and its tests.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: maintainer review and CI.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: N/A.
target.author: coygeek
